steps.title: Translate the given information into two constraints
steps.content:
Tanya's trip involves two key pieces of information:
  • Time: The drive took 5 hours total, so the first equation is x + y = 5
  • Distance: The trip was 290 miles total. We'll use distance = speed × time to build the second equation

steps.title: Build the distance equation using distance = speed × time
steps.content:
For each segment of the trip:
  • Highway: 65 mph × x hours = 65x miles
  • Local roads: 30 mph × y hours = 30y miles
Since the total distance is 290 miles:
$$65x + 30y = 290$$
Pairing this with the time equation $x + y = 5$ gives the listed system $65x + 30y = 290$ and $x + y = 5$, so B is correct.
